title: Pre-Market Briefing - 2026-07-13
markdown:
# Pre-Market Briefing

## 1) Market Tone
- **Confirmed:** Friday’s market finished higher, with the S&P 500 up **0.42%**, Nasdaq up **0.29%**, and Dow up **0.29%** (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** This morning futures were lower as the U.S. and Iran continued to trade strikes; the email cites early trade with the S&P 500 down **0.5%** and Nasdaq down **1.4%** (The Motley Fool).
- **Interpretation:** Risk tone looks shaky into the open, with geopolitics and inflation data likely dominating pre-open positioning.

## 2) Macro And Calendar
- **Confirmed:** **CPI** is due **Tuesday** and is expected to show **3.8% YoY** vs. **4.2%** in May; core CPI is expected to edge down to **2.8% YoY** from **2.9%** (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** **PPI** follows **Wednesday**; May was cited at **6.5%** YoY (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** Kevin Warsh testifies before Congress on **Tuesday and Wednesday**, following the inflation releases (The Motley Fool).
- **Interpretation:** Inflation prints plus testimony could matter for rate-hike expectations and morning sector rotations.

## 3) Earnings And Company News
- **Confirmed:** **NFLX** reports Q2 update on **Thursday**; the email highlights management’s push to **double annual advertising revenue** and says the stock has fallen in 2026 despite a good start to the year (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** **ASML** reports Q2 earnings **Wednesday morning** after beating expectations and lifting full-year sales guidance in Q1; factory expansion is key because of AI-driven chip orders for 2027 (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** **ISRG** reports Q2 figures **Thursday**; the email says watch for hospital upgrades to da Vinci 5 and AI tools (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** **USB** reports **Thursday** after Q1 net interest income rose **4.1% YoY**; management expects full-year revenue growth of **4% to 6%** (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** Major banks reporting **Tuesday** include **JPM**, **WFC**, **BAC**, **GS**, and **C** (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** **PGR** updates **Wednesday** after Q1 showed strong policy growth and net income/EPS both up close to **10% YoY** (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** **PLD** reports **Thursday**; the email says the year has been dominated by land acquisitions tied to its push into data centers (The Motley Fool).

## 4) Top Movers And Catalysts
- **Confirmed:** **SKHYV** climbed **13%** on its IPO debut Friday, though it fell in morning trading today (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** The email links broad market caution to inflation and geopolitical headlines rather than to a single stock-specific shock (The Motley Fool).
- **Interpretation:** The most actionable catalysts into the open are likely the upcoming inflation data and the earnings cluster in banks, semis, and software-ish/automation names.

## 5) Ranked Buy Candidates
> Only using the email material; no price-based confirmation or technicals were provided.

### 1. **ASML**
- **Catalyst:** Q2 earnings Wednesday morning; Q1 beat plus higher full-year sales target; AI-driven chip orders and factory expansion are the key follow-through themes (The Motley Fool).
- **Why it might matter for a swing trade:** A beat/raise setup could keep the AI capex theme active and attract momentum if guidance confirms demand.
- **Invalidation:** Any sign factory expansion or order visibility is not keeping up with AI demand expectations, or management sounds cautious on 2027 orders.
- **Check before entry:** Confirm the actual earnings release, guidance tone, and whether the post-earnings reaction supports continuation rather than fade.

### 2. **NFLX**
- **Catalyst:** Q2 update Thursday; management is chasing a doubling in annual ad revenue, and the stock has fallen in 2026 despite a good start to the year (The Motley Fool).
- **Why it might matter for a swing trade:** If ad growth or monetization commentary improves, the stock could respond as a recovery/turnaround name.
- **Invalidation:** Weak ad progress, disappointing engagement, or guidance that fails to support the ad-revenue ambition.
- **Check before entry:** Verify whether the update is enough to re-rate the stock or whether expectations are already too high.

### 3. **PGR**
- **Catalyst:** Wednesday update after strong Q1 policy growth and ~10% YoY gains in net income and EPS (The Motley Fool).
- **Why it might matter for a swing trade:** Consistent fundamental execution can support a steady trend continuation if the update confirms momentum.
- **Invalidation:** Any slowdown in policy growth or profitability versus the recent pace.
- **Check before entry:** Need the actual update and whether guidance or operating trends remain intact.

### 4. **ISRG**
- **Catalyst:** Thursday earnings; watch for hospital upgrades to da Vinci 5 and AI tools (The Motley Fool).
- **Why it might matter for a swing trade:** Positive product adoption commentary can support a premium-growth name.
- **Invalidation:** Slower-than-expected upgrade progress or lack of evidence that AI tools are moving the story.
- **Check before entry:** Confirm adoption metrics and whether the report meaningfully changes expectations.

### 5. **PLD**
- **Catalyst:** Thursday earnings with the data center expansion theme tied to land acquisitions (The Motley Fool).
- **Why it might matter for a swing trade:** Data center exposure can attract theme buying if management validates the strategy.
- **Invalidation:** If acquisitions don’t translate into visible progress or the data center push sounds less compelling than expected.
- **Check before entry:** Need confirmation that the earnings call reinforces the data-center narrative.

## 6) Swing Setup Board
| Ticker | Setup Type | Idea | Trigger | Stop / Invalidation | First Target Logic | Sizing Caution | Pass If |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **ASML** | Catalyst Continuation | Post-earnings continuation if Q2 and guidance are strong | Hold/add only after earnings confirms beat/raise tone | Weak factory expansion commentary or cautious AI-order outlook | Look for continuation off earnings gap/reaction, not before | Earnings gap risk is high; use smaller size | If reaction is muted or guidance is merely in-line |
| **NFLX** | Reversal Bounce | Turnaround attempt if ad-revenue progress is better than feared | Only if earnings/commentary re-energizes the ad thesis | Weak ad monetization or no improvement in forward tone | First target would be a follow-through move after the post-earnings repricing | High volatility into earnings; avoid oversized entries | If the report doesn’t change sentiment |
| **PGR** | Trend Pullback | Steady insurer trend continuation if fundamentals stay strong | Confirmation that policy growth and earnings momentum persist | Any sign of slowing growth or margin pressure | Target is a continuation of the existing earnings uptrend | More defensive, but still report-risk sensitive | If the update is just “fine” with no new catalyst |
| **ISRG** | Breakout / Momentum | Could work if da Vinci 5 and AI commentary surprise positively | Post-earnings strength and constructive guidance | Weak adoption data or disappointing product commentary | Momentum continuation if market likes the growth narrative | Premium multiple names can reverse fast on disappointment | If adoption/AI updates are vague |
| **PLD** | Catalyst Continuation | Data-center theme continuation | Reinforcement of land-acquisition/data-center strategy in earnings | Strategy sounds slow, expensive, or less concrete | Initial target is theme follow-through on confirmation | REITs can be sensitive to rates and macro headlines | If the call doesn’t validate the data-center angle |

## 7) Setup Watch
- **Confirmed:** **JPM**, **WFC**, **BAC**, **GS**, **C** all report Tuesday; that can affect financials broadly (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** **USB** reports Thursday after solid Q1 NII trends and full-year revenue growth guidance (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** **ASML**, **NFLX**, **ISRG**, **PGR**, and **PLD** are all near-term report names with potential post-earnings setups (The Motley Fool).
- **Interpretation:** Watch for post-earnings range expansion or fading depending on how the market absorbs CPI/PPI first.

## 8) Risk Watch
- **Confirmed:** Geopolitical tension is cited as a drag on futures this morning due to U.S.-Iran strike exchanges (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** CPI/PPI could shift rate expectations and pressure risk assets if inflation comes in hotter than expected (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** Banks are first up in the earnings season, which can reset sentiment for financials and cyclicals (The Motley Fool).
- **Interpretation:** Avoid assuming overnight weakness is “buyable” without seeing how futures, inflation, and bank results line up.

## 9) Sector And Theme Watch
- **AI / semis:** **ASML** is directly tied to AI-driven chip orders and factory expansion (The Motley Fool).
- **Streaming / ad monetization:** **NFLX** ad revenue growth is the key theme (The Motley Fool).
- **Insurance / financials:** **PGR**, **USB**, and the big-bank group (**JPM**, **WFC**, **BAC**, **GS**, **C**) make financials a key early-week watch (The Motley Fool).
- **Medical robotics / automation:** **ISRG** may move on da Vinci 5 adoption and AI tooling (The Motley Fool).
- **Data centers / REITs:** **PLD** is linked to a data center expansion push (The Motley Fool).

## 10) Worth Reading
- **Confirmed:** “Netflix Heads Up the Week’s Team Rule Breakers Earnings” and the section on bank earnings and inflation are the most directly actionable parts of the email (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** The “Markets Uncertain as Inflation Looms” section gives the key calendar items and current market tone (The Motley Fool).

## 11) Open Questions
- Will **CPI** and **PPI** confirm the expected cooling, or surprise hotter and pressure the open?
- Can **NFLX** actually re-accelerate ad revenue enough to support the stock?
- Will **ASML**’s factory expansion commentary match the strength implied by AI chip orders?
- Do the bank reports on Tuesday validate or soften the market’s financials setup?
- Will **ISRG** show meaningful progress on da Vinci 5 upgrades and AI tools?
- Does **PLD**’s data center push sound like a durable growth driver or just a strategic talking point?

## Notable Noise
- **SKHYV** had a strong IPO debut Friday but was already weaker in morning trading today; too early and too unclear for a high-conviction trade idea (The Motley Fool).
- The email includes general investing commentary and promotional content that is not useful for pre-market trade decisions.
- No actual earnings results, revisions, or technical levels were provided, so all swing ideas remain conditional.
